hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is insufficient to achieve improved selectivity beyond native SST-14. A focused mutagenesis approach at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to break off-target interactions while preserving SSTR2 binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: To target non-pharmacophore positions that are likely contributing to off-target binding (e.g., SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved-pocket contacts).
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved off-target contacts while maintaining flexibility and avoiding disruption of the pharmacophore.
